title: TSMC Plans Major AI Investment
original_title: TSMC optimistic about AI growth and will up investment in plants
sentences: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company announced ambitious plans to expand its artificial intelligence chip production.
The company expects demand for AI processors to increase dramatically over the next five years.
TSMC will invest over twenty billion dollars in new fabrication plants across Taiwan and abroad.
Artificial intelligence technology requires specialized chips that only a few companies can produce.
TSMC currently manufactures more than sixty percent of the world's advanced semiconductors.
The expansion will create thousands of new jobs for engineers and technicians in Taiwan.
Company executives say that AI will transform industries from healthcare to transportation.
Competitors in South Korea and the United States are also increasing their chip production capacity.
TSMC's investment demonstrates Taiwan's crucial role in the global technology supply chain.
Analysts predict that AI chip demand will reach one hundred billion dollars annually by 2030.

title: Military Conducts Island Defense Drill
original_title: Military drill has troops land in Green Island and Orchid Island
sentences: Taiwan's military conducted a large-scale defense exercise on Green Island and Orchid Island this week.
Hundreds of soldiers practiced landing operations and establishing defensive positions on the remote islands.
Green Island, located off Taiwan's southeastern coast, has historically served as a military outpost.
Orchid Island is home to the indigenous Tao people, who have lived there for centuries.
The drill simulated scenarios where troops must quickly reinforce outlying islands during emergencies.
Military officials emphasized that the exercises were part of regular defense preparedness training.
Soldiers used helicopters and transport boats to reach the islands during the early morning operation.
Local residents observed the military activities with interest, as similar drills occur several times yearly.
The exercises tested communication systems between the islands and Taiwan's main military command center.
Defense analysts note that Taiwan regularly strengthens its military readiness across all territories.

title: Restaurant Owner's Soccer Celebration Goes Wrong
original_title: Restaurant boss and footie fan gets out flare and gets in trouble
sentences: A restaurant owner in Taipei faced legal trouble after igniting a flare inside his establishment during a soccer match.
The man was watching an important international game with friends when his team scored a winning goal.
Excited by the victory, he pulled out a sports flare and lit it, filling the restaurant with thick red smoke.
Panicked customers immediately evacuated the building, and several people reported difficulty breathing.
The fire department arrived within minutes and determined there was no actual fire, only the flare smoke.
Police officers issued the owner citations for violating fire safety regulations and creating a public disturbance.
The restaurant temporarily closed while ventilation systems cleared the remaining smoke from the premises.
Neighbors complained about the loud noise and smoke that spread to nearby buildings and streets.
Local officials reminded business owners that indoor pyrotechnics violate Taiwan's fire prevention laws.
The incident was captured on surveillance cameras and quickly shared on social media, attracting widespread attention.

title: Former Pro-Gamer Pursues Culinary Dreams
original_title: Former pro-gamer opens night market stand selling classic Thai Moo Ping
sentences: A former professional esports player has successfully transitioned from competitive gaming to the food industry.
After retiring from professional gaming at age twenty-eight, he discovered a passion for Thai cuisine.
He spent two years learning traditional recipes from Thai chefs in Bangkok before opening his stall.
His night market stand specializes in Moo Ping, grilled pork skewers marinated in coconut milk and spices.
The stand has become incredibly popular, often attracting long lines of customers on weekend evenings.
He credits his gaming career with teaching him discipline, time management, and how to handle pressure.
Many of his former gaming fans now visit the night market to support his new business venture.
He plans to expand to three more locations across Taipei if his current stall continues performing well.
The young entrepreneur says that both gaming and cooking require creativity and attention to detail.
His success story has inspired other retired athletes to explore alternative career opportunities.

title: Ju Percussion Group Marks Four Decades
original_title: Ju Percussion Group celebrates 40 years in 2026
sentences: The Ju Percussion Group, one of Taiwan's most celebrated performing arts organizations, is celebrating its fortieth anniversary.
Founded in 1986, the group has performed in over forty countries across five continents.
Their unique style combines traditional Chinese percussion with contemporary choreography and storytelling.
The ensemble consists of twenty-eight musicians who train rigorously to achieve perfect synchronization.
Over the decades, the group has collaborated with international orchestras and avant-garde composers.
Their performances often address social themes such as environmental protection and cultural identity.
Young percussionists from Taiwan view the Ju Group as an inspiration and professional goal.
The anniversary celebration will include special concerts in Taipei, Kaohsiung, and Taichung.
Tickets for the commemorative performances sold out within hours of becoming available online.
Critics have praised the Ju Percussion Group for elevating Taiwanese arts on the world stage.

title: TV Commentator Attack Investigation Continues
original_title: Attack on political commentator linked to Chinese government
sentences: A prominent political commentator was assaulted outside his home in Taipei late yesterday evening.
The attack left the television host with minor injuries, and he was taken to a local hospital for treatment.
Police investigators are examining surveillance footage from the neighborhood to identify the assailant.
The commentator is well known for his critical analysis of cross-strait relations and Chinese government policies.
Several politicians and media organizations condemned the attack as an assault on freedom of speech.
The National Security Bureau is reportedly assisting local police with the ongoing investigation.
Colleagues at the television station expressed concern about the safety of journalists covering sensitive topics.
Press freedom organizations have called on authorities to ensure the protection of media professionals.
The commentator plans to return to his television program once he has fully recovered from his injuries.
This incident has reignited discussions about the security of public figures who express political opinions.
